Let $A_1, A_2, A_3$ be $2 \times 2$ matrices with entries in $\mathbb{C}$ (the
complex numbers). Let $\text{tr}$ denote the trace of a matrix (so $\text{tr}
\begin{pmatrix} a & b \\ c & d \end{pmatrix} = a + d$). Suppose $\{A_1,
A_2, A_3\}$ is closed under matrix multiplication (i.e., given $i, j$, there
exists $k$ such that $A_i A_j = A_k$), and $\text{tr}(A_1 + A_2 + A_3) \neq 3$.
Prove that there exists $i$ such that $A_i A_j = A_j A_i$ for all $j$ (here $i,
j$ are 1, 2 or 3).
